name: fsq-mac
summary: Agent-first macOS automation CLI for native app automation.

requires: Accessibility permissions granted to the host process

success_exceptions.behavior: prints raw shell script to stdout on success, or 'Script written to <path>' when --output is used

error_codes.description: No active session matches the requested ID
Session timed out or was cleaned up
Another session is already active; end it first
Appium server is not reachable; run 'mac doctor backend'
Bundle ID not found among running applications
Target window not found; it may still be loading
No element matches the locator; re-inspect and retry
Multiple elements match; narrow the locator
Ref was invalidated by a mutation; re-inspect
Element is visible but was not bound to a driver handle
Element failed actionability checks
Element bounds are degenerate or zero-area
Accessibility permission not granted
Safety classification blocks this action; use --allow-dangerous
Invalid argument supplied to command
An assert command's condition was not met
Trace step cannot be replayed
Typed text did not match expected value after verification
Driver operation timed out; retry or refresh snapshot
Command exceeded its timeout; increase --timeout or retry
Unexpected internal error

domains.name: doctor
domains.default_behavior: When no action is provided, the CLI runs all checks.

stability_notes.snapshot_contract: element.inspect is the preferred discovery primitive for element workflows
stability_notes.web_best_effort_signal: WEB_CONTENT_BEST_EFFORT means browser/web content is exposed through accessibility rather than DOM-native automation
stability_notes.reserved_code_rule: Agents should not branch on reserved codes until they become emitted in a future release

source_of_truth_note: This file is the machine-readable source of truth for agent-facing contract summaries. Human-facing docs should align with it.
